title: santolina melidensis
intro: Santolina melidensis, also known as lavender cotton or cotton lavender, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Santolina melidensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis. The plant can be prepared as a tea, oil, or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
